info.title: Spectra S3 API
info.description: Comprehensive API for Spectra S3 generated from request handlers.

post.description: Complete a blob upload that was written directly to cache, but has not yet been completed or aborted.This is an internal only request.

put.description: Create a bucket.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketPUT.html for AWS documentation).

delete.description: Delete a bucket.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketDELETE.html for AWS documentation).

head.description: Get bucket metadata key-values returned as HTTP headers.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketHEAD.html for AWS documentation).

put.description: Create an S3 object in a bucket.  Include the data for the object with your request.  It is recommended to use DS3 requests to create a PUT job before using a PUT request to put an object in the job rather than using this request stand alone in a strict S3 manner.  This is particularly important when putting many small objects or putting very large objects that must be broken up for physical placement.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectPUT.html for AWS documentation).

delete.description: Delete an S3 object in a bucket.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectDELETE.html for AWS documentation).

head.description: Get S3 object metadata key-values returned as HTTP headers.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectHEAD.html for AWS documentation).

post.description: Delete multiple S3 objects in a bucket.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/multiobjectdeleteapi.html for AWS documentation).

get.description: Get S3 objects in a bucket.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketGET.html for AWS documentation).
Get all buckets.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTServiceGET.html for AWS documentation).

Get the data for an S3 object in a bucket.  It is recommended to use DS3 requests to create a GET job before using a GET request to get an object in the job rather than using this request stand alone in a strict S3 manner.  This is particularly important when getting many small objects or getting very large objects that have been broken up for physical placement.  This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectGET.html for AWS documentation).

post.description: Create an ACL for a bucket, granting the specified permission to the specified group.  Only allowed for administrators and users with OWNER access to the bucket the ACL is being created for.
Create an ACL for a bucket, granting the specified permission to the specified user.  Only allowed for administrators and users with OWNER access to the bucket the ACL is being created for.
Create an ACL for a data policy, granting the specified group access to use the data policy.  Only allowed for administrators.
Create an ACL for a data policy, granting the specified user access to use the data policy.  Only allowed for administrators.
Create an ACL for all buckets current and future, granting the specified permission to the specified group.  Only allowed for administrators.
Create an ACL for all buckets current and future, granting the specified permission to the specified user.  Only allowed for administrators.
Create an ACL for all data policies current and future, granting the specified group access to use any data policy.  Only allowed for administrators.
Create an ACL for all data policies current and future, granting the specified user access to use any data policy.  Only allowed for administrators.

delete.description: Deletes the specified bucket ACL.  If the ACL being deleted is global (applies across all buckets), the operation is only allowed for administrators.  If the ACL being deleted is specific to a bucket, the operation is allowed for the bucket owners and administrators.

get.description: Get the specified bucket ACL.

delete.description: Deletes the specified data policy ACL.  Whether the ACL being deleted is global (applies to all data policies current and future) or not, this operation is only allowed for administrators.

Get bucket ACLs.

post.description: Create a cache throttle rule that sets the maximum percent of cache that will be allocated to active jobs based on bucket id, priority and/or request type. Objects within jobs must satisfy all applicable rules before cache will be allocated.

delete.description: Deletes the specified cache throttle rule.

get.description: Get the specified cache throttle rule.

put.description: Forces a full reclaim of all caches, and waits until the reclaim completes.  Cache contents that need to be retained since they're part of an active job will be retained.  Any cache contents that can be reclaimed will be.  This operation may take a very long time to complete, depending on how much of the cache can be reclaimed and how many blobs the cache is managing.

Gets all cache filesystems.

get.description: Get the capacity summary for the specified bucket on the specified storage domain.
Get the capacity summary for the specified storage domain.
Get the overall / system-wide capacity summary.
Get information on the data path backend.
Gets the tasks we are actively working on in the backend.  If the full details flag is specified, will also include all queued work in the backend.

put.description: Activate the data path and/or configure automatic data path activation.  Setting the auto activation timeout to null disables auto activation.  The unavailable media policy determines the policy for how to handle "temporarily unavailable" media when creating GET and VERIFY jobs.  For example, consider 2 cases: (1) a blob is on tapes A and B; (2) a blob is on tape B.  Consider that tape B is temporarily unavailable (e.g. its tape partition is quiesced, or its library is powered down).  With policy ALLOW, in case 1, we could service the job from either tape A or B, and in case 2, we would service the job from tape B.  With policy DISCOURAGED, in case 1, we would service the job from tape A, and in case 2, we would service the job from tape B.  With policy DISALLOW, in case 1, we would service the job from tape A, and in case 2, we would fail the job creation.

Verifies that the specified user is a member of the specified group.  If a user is not specified, it is assumed that the user making the request is the user to check for membership on.

Allocate a specific job chunk that is part of a PUT job before PUT'ing the objects to avoid 307 retries on the object PUTs, which adversely impact DS3 server performance and require you to perpetually provide the object data stream on PUTs for every retry.  The ID that must be supplied for this request is the job chunk's ID.  Use of this request handler is discouraged.  See GetJobChunksReadyForClientProcessingRequestHandler for the preferred way to get chunks to work on, which works for both GETs and PUTs, and has Spectra managing your working chunk window, rather than moving that complexity to you.

delete.description: Delegates to the CancelJobRequestHandler

get.description: Delegates to the CancelAllJobsRequestHandler

Forcibly cancels all jobs that are in progress, or, if filtering parameters are specified, cancels all jobs that are in progress that match the specified filtering criteria.  Any objects in the job that have been partially written to their permanent data stores (regardless as to whether or not they have been completely written to cache) will be deleted and any space object pieces consume on permanent data stores marked as eligible for reclamation.  For a less forced way to cancel jobs, look at TruncateAllJobsRequestHandler.

delete.description: Forcibly cancel a job that is in progress.  Any objects in the job that have been partially written to their permanent data stores (regardless as to whether or not they have been completely written to cache) will be deleted and any space object pieces consume on permanent data stores marked as eligible for reclamation.  For a less forced way to cancel jobs, look at TruncateJobRequestHandler.

get.description: Clears all canceled job history.
Clears all completed job history.

put.description: Closes an existing aggregating job so no more jobs may be appended to it.

Create a job to GET / read objects.  An XML payload must be sent to describe the GET job to create.  If not specified, the job priority and chunk client processing order guarantee will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for.The NONE chunk client processing order guarantee will achieve maximum performance and does not require pre-allocation in the cache, but requires the client of the job to get chunks as they become available, even if they become available out of order.  The job will time out and be canceled if there has been no activity for the job in at least 24 hours.  Any of the following is considered job activity: (i) I/O transfer to the client, or (ii) modify job requests (even if the modify request doesn't actually modify anything).  Folders are not allowed to be part of a GET job (since folders are always zero-length).  Objects that are zero-length that are requested as part of a GET job will be ignored (and will not be included in the resulting GET job created).

Create a job to PUT / write objects.  An XML payload must be sent to describe the PUT job to create.  If not specified, the job priority and write optimization will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for. In the response payload for creating a PUT job, the objects sent in the request will be broken up and organized into chunks.  Chunks must be sent by the client in order; however, objects within a given chunk may be sent in any order.  The job will time out and be canceled if there has been no activity for the job in at least 24 hours.  If a job is canceled due to lack of activity, it will be non-forcibly canceled, which ensures that any objects transmitted in their entirety by the client will be retained (and only objects that are incomplete will be canceled).  Any of the following is considered job activity: (i) I/O transfer from the client, or (ii) modify job requests (even if the modify request doesn't actually modify anything).  Folders (which are always zero-length) and zero-length objects must be explicitly PUT after creating a PUT job, which gives the client an opportunity to upload metadata for the folder or zero-length object at that time.  Note that, if the force flag is not specified, that VerifySafeToCreatePutJobRequestHandler will be invoked as a prerequisite step to creating the PUT job, to ensure it is safe to do so.

Create a job to verify objects.  An XML payload must be sent to describe the verify job to create.  If not specified, the job priority will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for.  A verify job reads data from tape and verifies that the CRC of the data read matches the expected CRC.  Verify jobs will ALWAYS read from tape - even if the data currently resides in cache.

delete.description: Deletes the job creation failure specified.

get.description: Gets the specified active job.

delete.description: Delegates to the TruncateJobRequestHandler.

get.description: Gets all in-progress jobs.
Gets the specified canceled job.
Gets all canceled jobs.
Gets the specified completed job.
Gets all completed jobs.
Get the specified job chunk (just the chunk information with no information about the objects in the chunk).
Get the specified job chunk (with the node and object information for the chunk).

Get all job chunks for a given job that are ready for client processing.  For PUT jobs, this will allocate a working window of job chunks if possible and return the job chunks that the client can upload.  Any chunk returned is fully allocated, meaning that you do not have to handle 307 retries on subsequent PUTs for said chunks, which adversely impacts DS3 server performance and requires you to perpetually attach the object data stream for every retry.  For GET jobs, this will respond with which job chunks have been loaded into cache and are ready for download.
Get the list of job creation failures.
Get all job entries for a given job.

Get the specified job.  The status field of the job returned will designate whether the job is IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, or CANCELED.  Jobs that have completed or been canceled will be automatically cleared after 30 days.  A job can be canceled explicitly by a user or automatically once internal timeouts are reached due to client inactivity.

delete.description: Cancel a job that is in progress.  Any objects in the job that have been written in their entirety to their permanent data stores will be retained.  For a more forced way to cancel jobs, look at CancelJobRequestHandler.

get.description: Get a progress summary of the specified job.

Returns a payload that represents a job that a client desires to replicate to another Black Pearl.  This payload is not intended to be consumed directly by the client, but rather, to be forwarded to another Black Pearl to replicate the job.
Get all in-progress jobs.  If the full details request parameter is specified, also includes recent completed and canceled jobs.

put.description: Delegates to the ModifyJobRequestHandler.
Modify a job that is in progress.

Replicates a job from another Black Pearl to this Black Pearl, ensuring that the object ids, blob ids, object versions, and blobbing structures for each object are identical, so that the objects are recognized as identical across the Black Pearls that replication is performed across.  Clients must feed the response payload returned by GetPutJobToReplicateRequestHandler on the source Black Pearl in as the request payload for theReplicatePutJobRequestHandler on the target Black Pearl.

Create a job to stage objects into the cache.  An XML payload must be sent to describe the GET job to create.  If not specified, the job priority and chunk client processing order guarantee will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for.

get.description: Delegates to the TruncateAllJobsRequestHandler.

Cancels all jobs that are in progress, or, if filtering parameters are specified, cancels all jobs that are in progress that match the specified filtering criteria.  Any objects in the job that have been written in their entirety to their permanent data stores will be retained.  For a more forced way to cancel jobs, look at CancelAllJobsRequestHandler.

put.description: Checks to see if the specified user can safely create a PUT job for the specified bucket at this time.  It is not safe to create a PUT job if at least one target is offline that we need to replicate to.

put.description: Modify the specified node.  The human and DNS names of the node can be modified.  If a DNS name isn't configured for a node, its data path IP address will be used when the TCP/IP address of the node must be given to a client.  If a DNS name is configured for a node, this DNS name (or IP address) will be used, regardless as to whether or not the data path IP address changes after the DNS name is configured.

put.description: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified.  Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job.  The lack of existence of an object or the lack of its having physical placement will not result in any exception.  If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis.  If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

Gets the physical placement of the objects specified.  Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job.  The lack of existence of an object or the lack of its having physical placement will not result in any exception.  If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis.  If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.
Undeletes an object on a versioned system if available. Can only be used on the most recent version of that object.

get.description: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified.  Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job.  A 404 not found error will be returned if one or more objects either (i) does not exist, or (ii) has no physical placement.  If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis.  If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

Gets the physical placement of the objects specified.  Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job.  A 404 not found error will be returned if one or more objects either (i) does not exist, or (ii) has no physical placement.  If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis.  If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

put.description: Cancel all pending pool imports.  Imports that have already begun cannot be cancelled.
Cancel a pending pool import on the specified pool.  If the import has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.
Cancel all pending pool verifications.  Verifies that have already begun cannot be cancelled.
Cancel a pending pool verification on the specified pool.  If the verify has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

Pool compaction is done automatically, periodically in the background.  Forces a compaction to be scheduled immediately for all eligible pools, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling.

Pool compaction is done automatically, periodically in the background.  When a pool is compacted, it is first checked to see if it can be reclaimed in its entirety and unassigned from its assigned storage domain, making it available to be allocated to any storage domain.  A pool can only be reclaimed in its entirety if it contains no data on it.  If the pool cannot be reclaimed in its entirety, its usage level will be checked and data that is eligible for deletion will be deleted as necessary to compact the pool down to where it has plenty of space for new data.  Forces a compaction to be scheduled immediately, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling of compaction.

Invoking this method should never be necessary unless an event is missed and thus we fail to update the pool environment.

put.description: Imports all foreign Black Pearl pools.  Note that this will not import a pool that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it.  If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified.  The storage domain to ingress the pool into may optionally be specified.  If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the pool into.  It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different.  Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.

Imports the specified foreign Black Pearl pool.  Note that this will not import a pool that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it.  If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified.  The storage domain to ingress the pool into may optionally be specified.  If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the pool into.  It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different.  Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
Modifies the quiesced state across all pools to the value specified.
Modify the specified pool partition.
Modify the specified pool.

Pool verification is done automatically, periodically in the background.  Forces a verification to be scheduled immediately for all eligible pools, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling.

Pool verification is done automatically, periodically in the background.  When a pool is verified, blobs that should be persisted on the pool are checked to ensure they do exist and are valid.  If a blob is missing or is otherwise invalid, a blob loss failure shall be generated.

Converts the specified storage domain to the specified DS3 target.  Once converted, there is no conversion back.  In converting the storage domain over, all blobs persisted locally will be migrated to be persisted at the target.

Forces a synchronous feature key validation.  Feature keys are validated regularly automatically, and invoking this request handler shouldn't ever be necessary to an end user, as validation will never re-enable a disabled key (it can only disable an enabled key).

get.description: Gets info about the current ABM (Advanced Bucket Management) configuration.
Gets all feature keys.
Gets a formal contract for the API that can be easily parsed.

Get information (and self-documentation) on all the request handlers supported by your DS3 server.  Note that the version for a request handler is always of the form X.Y, where X is the major revision and Yis the minor revision.  The major revision is always an integer.  The minor revision is always an MD5 checksum.
Gets the current system failures that must be fixed.

Get basic system information, including version and build information, and the system serial number.Note that the version is always of the form X.Y, where X is the MD5 checksum across all request handler majorrevisions and Y is the MD5 checksum across all request handler full versions (including the minor revision).

put.description: Resets and regenerates the instance identifier.  Resetting the instance identifier is only necessary in rare circumstances where there are two branches of an instance running at the same time.  Of of the two branches must have its instance identifier reset if the two instances are to replicate between each other.  The most common occurrence of this is when one instance is initially configured with dual copy where one copy goes on one library and the other copy goes on the other, then a new Black Pearl is added and one library and the new BP moved to another physical location.  In this case, both BPs would start with the same database and each would have the persistence rule and storage domain for the remote library converted to a replication rule.  This requires that one or both sites have their instance identifier reset first.

get.description: Verifies that the system appears to be online and functioning normally.  If critical components in thedata path front end are unresponsive, an error will be generated.

delete.description: Delete the specified offline tape drive.

get.description: Get the specified tape drive.

delete.description: Deletes the specified tape failure.
Deletes the specified tape partition failure.

Delete the specified offline tape partition.  Any tapes that had data on them in that partition will be de-associated from the partition.  Any tapes without data on them and tape drives that were associated with the partition will be deleted.

get.description: Get the specified tape partition.

put.description: Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected.  If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.
Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected within the storage domain specified for the blob payload included.  If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.
Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected within the storage domain specified.  If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.
Ejects the tape specified.  If the tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.

Forces the tape environment to be refreshed at the earliest convenience.  Note that the tape environment is updated automatically based on tape environment change events.  Invoking this method should never be necessary unless an event is missed and thus we fail to update the tape environment.

post.description: This is for testing purposes only e.g. test clients may want to be able to create a data policy without a real backend.  This will allow them to create a mocked tape backend they can create a storage domain using so that they can create the data policy to use.

put.description: Format all tapes that have not already been formatted and are not already in use by the system.  Tapes that contain data written externally of this product will be formatted even without the force flag.  Tapes that contain data written by this product (but not this specific appliance) will not be formatted unless the force flag is specified.  Tapes that contain data written by this appliance cannot be deleted in this manner.  You must delete the objects and/or buckets before you can format the tape they reside on.

Format the specified tape if it has not already been formatted and is not already in use by the system.  Tapes that contain data written externally of this product will be formatted even without the force flag.  Tapes that contain data written by this product (but not this specific appliance) will not be formatted unless the force flag is specified.  Tapes that contain data written by this appliance cannot be deleted in this manner.  You must delete the objects and/or buckets before you can format the tape they reside on.

get.description: Get the object pieces on the specified tape.
Get all tape density directives.
Get all tape drives.
Get all tape failures.
Get all tape libraries.
Get the specified tape library.
Get all tape partition failures.
Get the specified tape partition.
Get all tape partitions.
Get all tape partitions with full details in the response payload.
Get all tapes.

put.description: Imports all foreign Black Pearl tapes.  Note that this will not import tapes that have arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on them.  If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified.  The storage domain to ingress the tape into may optionally be specified.  If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the tape into.  It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different.  Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.

Imports the specified foreign Black Pearl tape.  Note that this will not import a tape that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it.  If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified.  The storage domain to ingress the tape into may optionally be specified.  If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the tape into.  It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different.  Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
Inspect all tapes that are not already queued to be inspected.
Inspect the specified tape if it has not already been queued to be inspected.
Manually mark a tape to be included in the next compaction cycle.
Modify the specified property for all tape partitions.
Modify the specified tape drive.
Modify the specified tape partition.
Modify the specified tape.
Onlines all tapes that are eligible to be onlined.  Tapes that show up in import/export slots must be onlined before they can be used.  Onlining a tape moves it to a storage slot.
Onlines the tape specified.  Tapes that show up in import/export slots must be onlined before they can be used.  Onlining a tape moves it to a storage slot.
Imports all non-Black Pearl foreign tapes.  Use ImportAllTapesRequestHandler to import all Black Pearl foreign tapes.
Imports the specified non-Black Pearl foreign tapes.  Use ImportTapeRequestHandler to import Black Pearl foreign tapes.
Tests the specified tape drive.
Verifies the media contents across all tapes (this may take a very long time).
Verifies the media contents on the specified tape.

Forces the target environment to be refreshed immediately and synchronously.  Note that the target environment is updated automatically based on a polling interval, so manual refreshes shouldn't be necessary unless there is an impatient consumer.
